forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,47 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '80'
+project: mirdan-install
+title: Tool CLIs don't install skills at user scope; code-context omits explore
+---
+Two confirmed regressions from the Profile migration (card 01KT7A4D44637D9Z1THZX6DASP), reproduced in an isolated temp HOME/CWD against the built `shelltool`/`code-context` binaries.
+
+## Bug 1 — `init user` installs NO skills (all three tool CLIs)
+`shelltool init user` registers the MCP server and the Bash-deny but deploys zero skills; `~/.skills` and `~/.claude/skills` are empty. Cause: each CLI's `project_scoped_skills(scope)` returns `Some(selector)` for Project/Local but **`None` for User** (`apps/shelltool-cli/src/commands/registry.rs:50`, and the equivalents in `apps/kanban-cli/src/commands/registry.rs` and `apps/code-context-cli/src/commands/registry.rs`). Added in card 4 "matching prior per-CLI behavior." At Project/Local scope skills DO deploy, so it looks inconsistent.
+
+**Decision (user):** remove the gate — skills must deploy at User scope too (into the global store `~/.skills` + `~/.claude/skills`), making `init user` a full configuration. This brings the tool CLIs in line with sah's `Selector::All` (which already deploys at every scope).
+
+### Fix
+- Delete `project_scoped_skills`'s `InitScope::User => None` gate in all three tool CLIs; return the skill selector at every scope. (Keep one helper or inline per CLI — whichever is cleaner now that it no longer branches on scope.)
+- Update each CLI's now-wrong unit test `test_user_scope_registers_mcp_but_deploys_no_skills` to assert skills ARE selected at User scope (rename accordingly). Keep `test_local_scope_deploys_skills`.
+
+## Bug 2 — code-context CLI never ships the `explore` skill (any scope)
+code-context's profile is `Named(["code-context","lsp"])` — `explore` is absent, so it can't install. (`code-context` + `lsp` do deploy at Project/Local.)
+
+**Decision (user):** code-context installs **`code-context` + `explore` + `lsp` + `detected-projects`**.
+
+### Fix
+- Change the code-context profile selector to `Named(["code-context","explore","lsp","detected-projects"])` (`apps/code-context-cli/src/commands/registry.rs`). All four builtins exist under `builtin/skills/`.
+- Update the code-context profile-shape unit test to assert the new four-skill set.
+
+## Why the existing consistency test missed both
+`mirdan::install::profile_consistency_tests` (card 01KT7A69W5R05QAZ1JWTQ3HH6M) (a) only exercises Project/Local scope, never User, and (b) **reconstructs** each CLI's profile rather than importing the real `profile()` — so a missing skill in the real profile is mirrored in the reconstruction and passes. This is the drift gap that card's own reviewer flagged.
+
+## Tests (TDD — write these FIRST and watch them fail, per /tdd)
+Isolated, HOME+CWD-isolated production-path tests (no repo leakage), covering **both User and Project scope**:
+1. **User-scope deploy (regression for Bug 1):** run the real init path at User scope for shelltool and code-context against an isolated `$HOME`; assert each declared skill lands as `~/.skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (store) AND a `~/.claude/skills/<name>` symlink (not a copy), and the MCP server is registered in the user-scope config. Must FAIL before the gate removal.
+2. **Project-scope deploy:** same assertions rooted at an explicit `<root>/.skills/` + `<root>/.claude/skills/`, no CWD access.
+3. **code-context skill set (regression for Bug 2):** assert the deployed set is exactly `{code-context, explore, lsp, detected-projects}` at both scopes. Must FAIL before the selector change.
+4. **Close the drift gap:** the test must assert against the **real** CLI skill expectations, not a private reconstruction. Since mirdan can't depend on the app crates, either (a) move these as integration tests into each app crate (`apps/*/tests/`) calling the real `commands::registry::profile(scope)` + `mirdan::install::init_profile`, or (b) assert the real builtin skills named by each profile actually resolve and deploy. Prefer (a) — it imports the true profile and can never drift.
+
+## Done when
+- `init user` and `init project`/`local` for shelltool, kanban-cli, code-context all deploy their declared skills (store + symlink) and register MCP — verified by the new tests.
+- code-context deploys exactly code-context + explore + lsp + detected-projects.
+- New tests fail before the fix and pass after; they drive the REAL profile, not a reconstruction.
+- `cargo build --workspace` green; clippy clean; full suite green.
+
+(Minor, optional: the reporter prints "Deployed 1 skill(s) to claude-code" where the count is the number of agent targets, not skills — mislabeled. Fix the wording if trivial while in here, otherwise leave a note.)
+
+#bug #mirdan #init #cli
\ No newline at end of file

-## Bug 1 — `init user` installs NO skills (all three tool CLIs)

-### Fix

-- Update each CLI's now-wrong unit test `test_user_scope_registers_mcp_but_deploys_no_skills` to assert skills ARE selected at User scope (rename accordingly). Keep `test_local_scope_deploys_skills`.
-
-## Bug 2 — code-context CLI never ships the `explore` skill (any scope)
-code-context's profile is `Named(["code-context","lsp"])` — `explore` is absent, so it can't install. (`code-context` + `lsp` do deploy at Project/Local.)
-
-**Decision (user):** code-context installs **`code-context` + `explore` + `lsp` + `detected-projects`**.
-
-### Fix
-- Change the code-context profile selector to `Named(["code-context","explore","lsp","detected-projects"])` (`apps/code-context-cli/src/commands/registry.rs`). All four builtins exist under `builtin/skills/`.
-- Update the code-context profile-shape unit test to assert the new four-skill set.
-
-## Why the existing consistency test missed both
-`mirdan::install::profile_consistency_tests` (card 01KT7A69W5R05QAZ1JWTQ3HH6M) (a) only exercises Project/Local scope, never User, and (b) **reconstructs** each CLI's profile rather than importing the real `profile()` — so a missing skill in the real profile is mirrored in the reconstruction and passes. -
-## Tests (TDD — write these FIRST and watch them fail, per /tdd)
-Isolated, HOME+CWD-isolated production-path tests (no repo leakage), covering **both User and Project scope**:
-1. **User-scope deploy (regression for Bug 1):** run the real init path at User scope for shelltool and code-context against an isolated `$HOME`; assert each declared skill lands as `~/.skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (store) AND a `~/.claude/skills/<name>` symlink (not a copy), and the MCP server is registered in the user-scope config. Must FAIL before the gate removal.
-2. **Project-scope deploy:** same assertions rooted at an explicit `<root>/.skills/` + `<root>/.claude/skills/`, no CWD access.
-3. **code-context skill set (regression for Bug 2):** assert the deployed set is exactly `{code-context, explore, lsp, detected-projects}` at both scopes. Must FAIL before the selector change.
-4. **Close the drift gap:** the test must assert against the **real** CLI skill expectations, not a private reconstruction. Since mirdan can't depend on the app crates, either (a) move these as integration tests into each app crate (`apps/*/tests/`) calling the real `commands::registry::profile(scope)` + `mirdan::install::init_profile`, or (b) assert the real builtin skills named by each profile actually resolve and deploy. Prefer (a) — it imports the true profile and can never drift.
-
-## Done when
-- `init user` and `init project`/`local` for shelltool, kanban-cli, code-context all deploy their declared skills (store + symlink) and register MCP — verified by the new tests.
-- code-context deploys exactly code-context + explore + lsp + detected-projects.
-- New tests fail before the fix and pass after; they drive the REAL profile, not a reconstruction.
-- `cargo build --workspace` green; clippy clean; full suite green.
-
-(Minor, optional: the reporter prints "Deployed 1 skill(s) to claude-code" where the count is the number of agent targets, not skills — mislabeled. Fix the wording if trivial while in here, otherwise leave a note.)
-
-#bug #mirdan #init #cli

@@ -44,4 +44,12 @@

 (Minor, optional: the reporter prints "Deployed 1 skill(s) to claude-code" where the count is the number of agent targets, not skills — mislabeled. Fix the wording if trivial while in here, otherwise leave a note.)

-#bug #mirdan #init #cli
\ No newline at end of file
+#bug #mirdan #init #cli
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-04 08:38)
+
+Both bugs are correctly fixed and verified. Build green, clippy clean (`shelltool-cli`, `kanban-cli`, `code-context-cli`, `mirdan`), all registry tests pass; the four-skill set and User-scope skill selection assertions are genuine regressions that gate the fix. The optional reporter mislabel was also fixed — `install.rs:1082` now emits `skill_count` (`selected.len()`) instead of the target count. Skill-list reuse is clean: `skill.rs` builds its profile from `registry::skills_selector()` rather than re-listing the names. The drift gap is closed at the app layer: each CLI's new `registry.rs` integration test drives the **real** `profile(scope)` through `mirdan::install::init_profile` and the shared `test_support` deploy asserters.
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/mirdan/src/install.rs:5031-5121` — `profile_consistency_tests` still hardcodes **reconstructed** copies of each CLI's profile (`shelltool_profile`/`kanban_profile`/`code_context_profile` and the `expected_skills` lists at lines 5101/5107/5113/5119). These are currently in sync, but they are the exact drift source the task set out to eliminate: editing a real CLI `profile()` (e.g. adding a 5th code-context skill) will NOT break these mirror copies. The new app-crate tests now provide the authoritative anti-drift coverage, so these reconstructed skill-set lists are redundant and latently misleading. Suggestion: trim the reconstructed lists to the bare minimum needed for the mechanism/round-trip/scope-matrix coverage that legitimately must live in mirdan (which can't import the app crates), and add a comment pointing skill-set authority at the per-CLI `registry.rs` tests — or assert each reconstruction's skill set is merely "non-empty and resolves" rather than enumerating it, so it can't pretend to verify the real set.
+- [ ] `crates/mirdan/src/install.rs:5129` / `:5155` / `:5168` — `write_agents_config`, `assert_no_error`, and `read_json` in `profile_consistency_tests` are near-verbatim duplicates of the new public `test_support::write_single_agent_config`, `test_support::assert_no_init_error`, and `test_support`'s private `read_json` (the module already imports `super::test_support::MirdanConfigGuard`, so `test-support` is enabled in this build). `read_json` is byte-identical; `write_agents_config(dir)` is `write_single_agent_config(dir, dir)`; `assert_no_error` only adds a `label`. Suggestion: reuse the public helpers (pass `dir` for both root and home; add an optional label arg to `assert_no_init_error` or wrap it) and delete the inline copies so the two test homes can't drift.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -51,5 +51,5 @@
 Both bugs are correctly fixed and verified. Build green, clippy clean (`shelltool-cli`, `kanban-cli`, `code-context-cli`, `mirdan`), all registry tests pass; the four-skill set and User-scope skill selection assertions are genuine regressions that gate the fix. The optional reporter mislabel was also fixed — `install.rs:1082` now emits `skill_count` (`selected.len()`) instead of the target count. Skill-list reuse is clean: `skill.rs` builds its profile from `registry::skills_selector()` rather than re-listing the names. The drift gap is closed at the app layer: each CLI's new `registry.rs` integration test drives the **real** `profile(scope)` through `mirdan::install::init_profile` and the shared `test_support` deploy asserters.

 ### Warnings
-- [ ] `crates/mirdan/src/install.rs:5031-5121` — `profile_consistency_tests` still hardcodes **reconstructed** copies of each CLI's profile (`shelltool_profile`/`kanban_profile`/`code_context_profile` and the `expected_skills` lists at lines 5101/5107/5113/5119). These are currently in sync, but they are the exact drift source the task set out to eliminate: editing a real CLI `profile()` (e.g. adding a 5th code-context skill) will NOT break these mirror copies. The new app-crate tests now provide the authoritative anti-drift coverage, so these reconstructed skill-set lists are redundant and latently misleading. Suggestion: trim the reconstructed lists to the bare minimum needed for the mechanism/round-trip/scope-matrix coverage that legitimately must live in mirdan (which can't import the app crates), and add a comment pointing skill-set authority at the per-CLI `registry.rs` tests — or assert each reconstruction's skill set is merely "non-empty and resolves" rather than enumerating it, so it can't pretend to verify the real set.
-- [ ] `crates/mirdan/src/install.rs:5129` / `:5155` / `:5168` — `write_agents_config`, `assert_no_error`, and `read_json` in `profile_consistency_tests` are near-verbatim duplicates of the new public `test_support::write_single_agent_config`, `test_support::assert_no_init_error`, and `test_support`'s private `read_json` (the module already imports `super::test_support::MirdanConfigGuard`, so `test-support` is enabled in this build). `read_json` is byte-identical; `write_agents_config(dir)` is `write_single_agent_config(dir, dir)`; `assert_no_error` only adds a `label`. Suggestion: reuse the public helpers (pass `dir` for both root and home; add an optional label arg to `assert_no_init_error` or wrap it) and delete the inline copies so the two test homes can't drift.
\ No newline at end of file
+- [x] `crates/mirdan/src/install.rs` `profile_consistency_tests` — RESOLVED. The reconstructed `expected_skills` enumerations are removed. Each `CliProfile` now carries a single `probe_skill` used only to exercise the store+symlink deploy *mechanism*; the struct/module docs explicitly state the authoritative per-profile skill set is owned by the per-CLI `commands::registry` tests (which drive the real `profile(scope)`). No reconstructed list can pretend to verify the real set anymore.
+- [x] `crates/mirdan/src/install.rs` `profile_consistency_tests` helper duplication — RESOLVED. `write_agents_config`/`read_json` deleted; the module now reuses `crate::test_support::{write_single_agent_config, read_json, assert_no_init_error, MirdanConfigGuard}`. `read_json` was made `pub` in `test_support`. The stale in-crate `#[cfg(test)] mod test_support` (a duplicate `MirdanConfigGuard`) in install.rs was deleted, and `applier_tests`/`profile_tests`/`profile_consistency_tests` now import the single public scaffolding. mirdan's own `test-support` feature is enabled for its in-crate tests via a self dev-dependency in Cargo.toml. The thin `assert_no_error(label, phase, ...)` wrapper remains as a deliberate adapter over the shared `assert_no_init_error` (combines two label parts). All 387 mirdan tests + the three CLIs' bin tests pass; clippy clean; `cargo build --workspace` green.
\ No newline at end of file

 - Update each CLI's now-wrong unit test `test_user_scope_registers_mcp_but_deploys_no_skills` to assert skills ARE selected at User scope (rename accordingly). Keep `test_local_scope_deploys_skills`.

 ## Bug 2 — code-context CLI never ships the `explore` skill (any scope)

Two occurrences on a real user-global/project-local axis — rule-of-three says this is coincidence, not yet a pattern, and the explicit two-struct form is defensibly clearer than one parameterized helper, so this is optional. If a third scope variant ever appears, extract a shared `assert_skill_deployed(base, agent_dir, skill, label)` + `assert_mcp_registered(mcp_path, server, label)` pair and have both structs call it.

@@ -9,54 +9,52 @@
 Two confirmed regressions from the Profile migration (card 01KT7A4D44637D9Z1THZX6DASP), reproduced in an isolated temp HOME/CWD against the built `shelltool`/`code-context` binaries.

 ## Bug 1 — `init user` installs NO skills (all three tool CLIs)
-`shelltool init user` registers the MCP server and the Bash-deny but deploys zero skills; `~/.skills` and `~/.claude/skills` are empty. Cause: each CLI's `project_scoped_skills(scope)` returns `Some(selector)` for Project/Local but **`None` for User** (`apps/shelltool-cli/src/commands/registry.rs:50`, and the equivalents in `apps/kanban-cli/src/commands/registry.rs` and `apps/code-context-cli/src/commands/registry.rs`). Added in card 4 "matching prior per-CLI behavior." At Project/Local scope skills DO deploy, so it looks inconsistent.
+`shelltool init user` registers the MCP server and the Bash-deny but deploys zero skills; `~/.skills` and `~/.claude/skills` are empty. Cause: each CLI's `project_scoped_skills(scope)` returns `Some(selector)` for Project/Local but **`None` for User**. Added in card 4 "matching prior per-CLI behavior." At Project/Local scope skills DO deploy, so it looks inconsistent.

-**Decision (user):** remove the gate — skills must deploy at User scope too (into the global store `~/.skills` + `~/.claude/skills`), making `init user` a full configuration. This brings the tool CLIs in line with sah's `Selector::All` (which already deploys at every scope).
+**Decision (user):** remove the gate — skills must deploy at User scope too (into the global store `~/.skills` + `~/.claude/skills`), making `init user` a full configuration. This brings the tool CLIs in line with sah's `Selector::All`.

 ### Fix
-- Delete `project_scoped_skills`'s `InitScope::User => None` gate in all three tool CLIs; return the skill selector at every scope. (Keep one helper or inline per CLI — whichever is cleaner now that it no longer branches on scope.)
-- Update each CLI's now-wrong unit test `test_user_scope_registers_mcp_but_deploys_no_skills` to assert skills ARE selected at User scope (rename accordingly). Keep `test_local_scope_deploys_skills`.
+- Delete `project_scoped_skills`'s `InitScope::User => None` gate in all three tool CLIs; return the skill selector at every scope.

 ## Bug 2 — code-context CLI never ships the `explore` skill (any scope)
-code-context's profile is `Named([\"code-context\",\"lsp\"])` — `explore` is absent, so it can't install. (`code-context` + `lsp` do deploy at Project/Local.)

+- Change the code-context profile selector to `Named(["code-context","explore","lsp","detected-projects"])`. All four builtins exist under `builtin/skills/`.
 - Update the code-context profile-shape unit test to assert the new four-skill set.

 ## Why the existing consistency test missed both
-`mirdan::install::profile_consistency_tests` (card 01KT7A69W5R05QAZ1JWTQ3HH6M) (a) only exercises Project/Local scope, never User, and (b) **reconstructs** each CLI's profile rather than importing the real `profile()` — so a missing skill in the real profile is mirrored in the reconstruction and passes. This is the drift gap that card's own reviewer flagged.
+`mirdan::install::profile_consistency_tests` (a) only exercised Project/Local scope, never User, and (b) **reconstructed** each CLI's profile rather than importing the real `profile()` — so a missing skill is mirrored in the reconstruction and passes. The drift gap the card-7 reviewer flagged.

-## Tests (TDD — write these FIRST and watch them fail, per /tdd)
-Isolated, HOME+CWD-isolated production-path tests (no repo leakage), covering **both User and Project scope**:
-1. **User-scope deploy (regression for Bug 1):** run the real init path at User scope for shelltool and code-context against an isolated `$HOME`; assert each declared skill lands as `~/.skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (store) AND a `~/.claude/skills/<name>` symlink (not a copy), and the MCP server is registered in the user-scope config. Must FAIL before the gate removal.
-2. **Project-scope deploy:** same assertions rooted at an explicit `<root>/.skills/` + `<root>/.claude/skills/`, no CWD access.
-3. **code-context skill set (regression for Bug 2):** assert the deployed set is exactly `{code-context, explore, lsp, detected-projects}` at both scopes. Must FAIL before the selector change.
-4. **Close the drift gap:** the test must assert against the **real** CLI skill expectations, not a private reconstruction. Since mirdan can't depend on the app crates, either (a) move these as integration tests into each app crate (`apps/*/tests/`) calling the real `commands::registry::profile(scope)` + `mirdan::install::init_profile`, or (b) assert the real builtin skills named by each profile actually resolve and deploy. Prefer (a) — it imports the true profile and can never drift.
+## Tests (TDD — write FIRST and watch fail)
+Isolated, HOME+CWD-isolated production-path tests covering **both User and Project scope**:
+1. User-scope deploy regression: skill lands as `~/.skills/<name>/SKILL.md` + `~/.claude/skills/<name>` symlink, MCP registered. Must FAIL before gate removal.
+2. Project-scope deploy: same, rooted at explicit `<root>`, no CWD access.
+3. code-context skill set: exactly `{code-context, explore, lsp, detected-projects}`. Must FAIL before selector change.
+4. Close drift gap: drive the REAL `profile(scope)` as `apps/*/tests/`-style integration tests, not a reconstruction.

-- New tests fail before the fix and pass after; they drive the REAL profile, not a reconstruction.

+1. **User-scope deploy (regression for Bug 1):** run the real init path at User scope for shelltool and code-context against an isolated `$HOME`; assert each declared skill lands as `~/.skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (store) AND a `~/.claude/skills/<name>` symlink (not a copy), and the MCP server is registered in the user-scope config. Must FAIL before the gate removal.
+2. **Project-scope deploy:** same assertions rooted at an explicit `<root>/.skills/` + `<root>/.claude/skills/`, no CWD access.
+3. **code-context skill set (regression for Bug 2):** assert the deployed set is exactly `{code-context, explore, lsp, detected-projects}` at both scopes. Must FAIL before the selector change.
+4. **Close the drift gap:** the test must assert against the **real** CLI skill expectations, not a private reconstruction. Since mirdan can't depend on the app crates, either (a) move these as integration tests into each app crate (`apps/*/tests/`) calling the real `commands::registry::profile(scope)` + `mirdan::install::init_profile`, or (b) assert the real builtin skills named by each profile actually resolve and deploy. Prefer (a) — it imports the true profile and can never drift.

 ## Done when
-- `init user` and `init project`/`local` for all three CLIs deploy their declared skills (store + symlink) and register MCP — verified by the new tests.
-- code-context deploys exactly the four skills.
-- New tests fail before the fix and pass after; they drive the REAL profile.

DECLINED (kept as two explicit structs): the reviewer rated this a 2-occurrence rule-of-three coincidence, not a pattern, and explicitly noted the explicit two-struct form is "defensibly clearer" than one parameterized helper. The two structs sit on a real user-global vs project-local axis (global `~/.skills` + global `.fake/mcp.json` vs explicit-root `<root>/.skills` + project `.mcp.json`); collapsing them now would obscure that axis.
